expectations.text: References the existing feature-dev skill directly rather than answering generically.
expectations.evidence: The response opens by saying it inspected skills/feature-dev/SKILL.md and then cites the description, Light Track, Phase 1 track-selection bullets, and the existing When Not To Use section.

expectations.text: Avoids asking blocking clarifying questions for this well-specified task.
expectations.evidence: It asks no blocking questions and goes straight into a four-point change list for the repo file.

expectations.text: Proposes concrete description and body changes, including a scoped 'When not to use this skill' addition.
expectations.evidence: It proposes replacement description text, revised Light Track wording, revised Phase 1 bullets, and a sharper When Not To Use This Skill section.

expectations.text: Scales the workflow to the task instead of insisting on a full multi-phase approval process.
expectations.evidence: The answer stays focused on a small targeted consistency pass instead of asking for broad discovery, architecture choices, or explicit approval.

claims.claim: The main remaining scope leak is in the trigger text and Light Track language.
claims.evidence: The response identifies both the description and Light Track as the remaining places that still invite smaller work.
